# Example roles

The flexibility of Boundary's permissions model lets you create custom roles and permissions tailored for your organization's specific needs.
This topic provides sample roles based on common Boundary use cases and management needs.
You can use the permissions below to create roles, or you can use them as starting points and modify them to meet your specific needs.

## Administrative roles

Administrative roles have full access over all resources in a given scope.
You can configure administrators for global, org, or project scopes.

| Role | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| [Global Admin](#global-admin) | Full administrative access to all resources in Boundary |
| [Org Admin](#org-admin) | Administrative access to all resources in an org scope |
| [Project Admin](#project-admin) | Administrative access to all resources in a project scope |

### Global Admin

@include 'roles/global-admin.mdx'

### Org Admin

@include 'roles/org-admin.mdx'

### Project Admin

@include 'roles/project-admin.mdx'

## Resource management roles

Resource management roles can manage specific Boundary resources such as sessions, targets, or credentials.

| Role | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| [Org Access](#org-access) | Perform actions on an org scope, but not the resources the org contains |
| [Project Access](#project-access) | Perform actions on a project scope, but not the resources the project contains |
| [Session Manager](#session-manager) | View and cancel sessions |
| [Target Manager](#target-manager) | Create and manage targets |
| [Host Resource Manager](#host-resource-manager) | Manage hosts and host catalogs |
| [Credential Manager](#credential-manager) | Manage credentials and credential stores |
| [User Manager](#user-manager) | Manage users, groups, and accounts |
| [Auth Method Manager](#auth-method-manager) | Manage auth methods and managed groups |

### Org Access

@include 'roles/org-access.mdx'

### Project Access

@include 'roles/project-access.mdx'

### Session Manager

@include 'roles/session-manager.mdx'

### Target Manager

### Host Resource Manager

@include 'roles/host-resource-manager.mdx'

### Credential Manager

@include 'roles/credential-manager.mdx'

### User Manager

@include 'roles/user-manager.mdx'

### Auth Method Manager

@include 'roles/auth-method-manager.mdx'

## Audit roles

Audit roles can view information about Boundary resources, but they cannot modify them.

| Role | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| [Session Auditor](#session-auditor) | View session recordings |
| [Resource Auditor](#resource-auditor) | View resources, but cannot modify them |

### Session Auditor

@include 'roles/session-auditor.mdx'

### Resource Auditor

@include 'roles/resource-auditor.mdx'

## End-user roles

End users can use Boundary to connect to targets, but they cannot modify or manage resources.

| Role | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| [User](#user) | Connect to targets, but cannot modify or manage resources |

### User

@include 'roles/user.mdx'

## More information

- To better understand Boundary's permissions model, refer to [Permissions in Boundary](/boundary/docs/rbac).
- To learn more about the permissions you can assign to Boundary principals, refer to [Assignable permissions](/boundary/docs/rbac/assignable-permissions).
- To learn more about grant strings and view example formats, refer to [Permission grant formats](/boundary/docs/rbac/permission-grant-formats).
- To view a cheat sheet to help you manage your permissions, refer to [Resource tables](/boundary/docs/rbac/resource-table).
- To view a cheat sheet to help you create roles, refer to [Example roles](/boundary/docs/rbac/example-roles).

## Next steps

Refer to [Manage access with roles](/boundary/docs/rbac/manage-roles) for more information about creating roles, assigning principals and grants to roles, and adding grant scopes.
